untranslatable_reason: This signal requires tracking two separate assets (substitute products) and their relative price divergence net of transaction costs (carrying charges, shipping, inspection, commissions). The backtest engine appears single-asset focused with no multi-leg arbitrage support, no cross-asset price comparison operators, and no way to model dynamic transaction cost components. Standard single-asset indicators cannot capture the spread between two physical products or measure composite transaction costs dynamically.
translation_notes: KAUFMAN_DRY_6 is a pairs/arbitrage strategy requiring: (1) dual-asset tracking, (2) spread calculation between substitute products, (3) transaction cost modeling (carrying, shipping, inspection, commissions), (4) synchronized entry/exit across long/short legs. None of these capabilities exist in the allowed indicator set. Implementation requires custom multi-leg arbitrage logic outside this single-asset rule framework.
